entries.d: a hat with a flat part at the front
entries.s: He wears a blue baseball _____ on his head with a stiff peak at the front to keep the sun off his eyes.

entries.d: something you wear over your face
entries.s: The masked superhero wears a black cloth _____ over his eyes and nose to hide his identity.

entries.d: a short coat you wear outside
entries.s: Put on your warm waterproof _____ with a zip because it is cold and windy outside.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with JACKET?

entries.d: a nice shirt for girls or women
entries.s: She wears a smart white _____ for girls with small buttons and a soft fabric to school.

entries.d: clothes that cover both legs

entries.d: a top with a hood you pull over your head
entries.s: He always wears his favourite grey _____ with a hood at the back that he can pull up over his head.

entries.d: a long piece of cloth you wear on your back
entries.s: The comic book superhero flies through the sky with a long red _____ flowing behind his back.

entries.d: soft clothes you wear in bed
entries.s: I put on my cotton _____ — a top and trousers for sleeping — before I go to bed at night.

entries.d: thin clothes that cover your legs and feet
entries.s: She wears warm grey _____ covering her legs from her feet to her waist under her skirt in winter.

entries.d: things you wear on your feet outside
entries.s: I need new black school _____ because my old ones are too small and my toes are hurting.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with SHOES?

entries.d: strong shoes that cover your ankles
entries.s: When it rains heavily, I wear my tall waterproof rubber _____ so my feet stay dry.

entries.d: soft shoes for running and sports
entries.s: You need sporty _____ with rubber soles for PE class today so you can run.

entries.d: a long band you wear around your waist
entries.s: His trousers are too big and keep falling down, so he needs a leather _____ around his waist.

entries.d: an opening or empty space in something
entries.s: There is a big _____ in my left sock and my big toe comes out through it.

entries.d: any thing, used in questions
entries.s: Is there _____ at all in your empty pockets? Let me check.

entries.d: to take something and give it back later
entries.s: Can I _____ your warm jacket for a few minutes? I am cold and I will give it back later.

entries.d: to be the right size for someone

entries.d: to put on clothes to see if they fit
entries.s: Can I _____ this jacket in the shop before I decide to buy it?

entries.d: to have clothes on your body

entries.d: to feel pain in your body

entries.d: a short text with rhythm and feeling
entries.s: She reads a short rhyming _____ about a cat and a bat to the whole class.

entries.d: to touch someone so they laugh

entries.d: a person, but you don't know who
entries.s: _____ — we don't know who — left a black jacket behind in the classroom after the lesson.

entries.d: in the opposite direction, from end to start
entries.s: Can you say the alphabet _____, from Z to A, without making any mistakes?

entries.d: making you feel happy and full of energy

entries.d: this evening, this night

entries.d: let us leave this place quickly
